symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained fast, pounding heartbeat not settling with rest (patient-reported ~130)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic overdrive produces sinus tachycardia, but this is non-specific.
A persistent fast rate not settling with rest requires ECG rhythm characterisation before attribution.
Tachycardia accompanies many ACS presentations but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest pressure / tightness during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest pressure is an ACS-until-excluded symptom; telehealth cannot obtain the ECG/troponin that exclude it.
Non-ischaemic chest pressure reproduced by hyperventilation — but only assignable after cardiac exclusion.
Orthostatic intolerance is a core feature of the autonomic picture.
Presyncope can equally signal a haemodynamically significant arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Facial/neck flushing with diaphoresis in a cool room (vasomotor instability)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paroxysmal flushing/sweating/tachycardia overlaps; biochemical exclusion required, not remote.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shortness of breath with rapid breathing (hyperventilation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation reproduces the chest pressure and accompanies acute arousal.
Rapid breathing is a hallmark of hyperventilation/panic — must not be used to dismiss cardiac causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved oxygen saturation (SpO2 98%, patient-reported on a home oximeter)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal saturations do NOT exclude ACS or arrhythmia; must not be used for false reassurance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms triggered by an acute experienced-as-discriminatory social encounter, on a recurrent stress-linked pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear psychosocial trigger and recurrent pattern support the functional diagnosis — but a trigger does not exclude a concurrent cardiac event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension on formal lying-and-standing BP (145/95 supine to 100/65 standing) with postural HR rise to ~145

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective orthostatic drop with postural tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI can only infer orthostatic intolerance from reported near-syncope, never from a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular sinus rhythm on ECG correlating with posture/trigger; parasympathetic blunting (HR fails to recover after 15 min supine)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG/rhythm strip is required to exclude a primary arrhythmia and confirm sinus tachycardia — never available in real time via telehealth.
Sinus rhythm plus impaired parasympathetic recovery confirms the functional picture once arrhythmia is excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiac biomarkers / no ischaemic ECG changes (would exclude ACS)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are the tests that exclude ACS; their absence in telehealth is exactly why escalation is mandatory.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Undifferentiated acute cardiovascular presentation (chest pressure + palpitations + presyncope)
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome / dangerous arrhythmia (must be excluded)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This triad in an undifferentiated patient mandates in-person ECG and biomarkers before any non-cardiac label; it is the reason the case escalates rather than reassures.

Posture-correlated tachycardia, orthostatic hypotension, vasomotor instability and a psychosocial trigger fit functional dysautonomia — the eventual diagnosis, assignable only after cardiac exclusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to characterise the tachycardia and screen for ischaemia/arrhythmia; cannot be done via telehealth, so it drives the escalation.
Required to exclude ACS given chest pressure with palpitations.
Objectively documents the orthostatic drop; a clinician maneuver, not a telehealth measurement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Tilt-table testing and 24-hour Holter / HRV assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Outpatient confirmation of autonomic dysfunction after the acute cardiac exclusion — not part of the acute telehealth episode.
Considered on the differential given paroxysmal flushing/tachycardia/labile BP; arranged by the in-person team, not remotely.
